title: KtulueKit Config
description: Declarative desired-state config for KtulueKit-W11 automated installer

version.description: Schema version. Bump when breaking changes are made.

log_dir.description: Directory where timestamped result logs are written.

retry_count.description: How many times to retry a failed install before marking it failed.

default_timeout_seconds.description: Per-package install timeout in seconds. Can be overridden per item.

default_scope.description: Default winget install scope. 'machine' requires admin.

extension_mode.description: 'force' writes registry policy (shows 'Managed by org'). 'url' opens Chrome Web Store for manual click.

upgrade_if_installed.description: If true and the check command passes (package is already installed), run 'winget upgrade' instead of skipping.

packages.description: Tier 1 — Winget packages. Winget natively skips already-installed apps.

commands.description: Tier 2 — Shell commands (npm, wsl, pip, etc.). Tool checks before running.

extensions.description: Tier 3 — Browser extensions installed via registry policy or URL.

profiles.description: Predefined installation profiles grouping package/command/extension IDs.

ids.description: List of package, command, and extension IDs to include in this profile.

snapshot.description: Present in snapshot files only. Records when and how the snapshot was generated.

machine.description: Hostname of the machine that generated the snapshot.

source_config.description: Absolute path to the reference config used.

tool_version.description: KtulueKit version that generated the snapshot.

id.description: Exact winget package ID. Used with 'winget install -e --id'.

name.description: Human-readable display name for logs and reports.

category.description: Category or classification for this package (e.g., 'developer', 'utility', 'runtime').

phase.description: Install phase. Lower phases run first. Items within the same phase run sequentially.

scope.description: Override the default install scope for this package. Use 'user' for apps like Spotify.

check.description: Shell command to detect if this package is already installed. Exit code 0 = installed (skip or upgrade). Non-zero = not installed, run winget.

version.description: Pin to a specific package version. Passed to winget as '--version'. Useful for runtimes where major-version bumps are undesirable.

reboot_after.description: If true, tool will prompt to reboot and resume after this package installs.

notes.description: Shown in dry-run output and logs. Use for known quirks or warnings.

id.description: Unique identifier for this command. Used in depends_on references.

name.description: Human-readable display name for logs and reports.

category.description: Category or classification for this command (e.g., 'developer', 'utility', 'runtime').

phase.description: Install phase. Should be >= the phase of all its dependencies.

check.description: Shell command to detect if this is already installed. Exit code 0 = installed, skip. Non-zero = not installed, run.

command.description: The shell command to run for installation.

depends_on.description: List of winget package IDs or command IDs that must have succeeded before this runs. If any dependency failed, this is skipped.

reboot_after.description: If true, tool will prompt to reboot and resume after this command runs.

on_failure_prompt.description: If the command fails, this message is printed as manual install guidance and the tool pauses for user acknowledgment. Supports multi-line via \n.

notes.description: Shown in dry-run output and logs.

scrape_url.description: Page URL to fetch HTML from to find the download link. Mutually exclusive with 'command'.

url_pattern.description: Go regex pattern to extract the download URL from the page HTML. Required when scrape_url is set.

install_args.description: Space-separated CLI flags for the installer (e.g. /S for silent NSIS). Simple flags only — no quoted tokens with spaces.

id.description: Unique identifier for this extension entry.

category.description: Category or classification for this extension (e.g., 'productivity', 'privacy', 'utility').

extension_id.description: Chrome Web Store extension ID (32-character lowercase string).

browser.description: Target browser. Determines the registry path or policy mechanism used.

mode.description: Override the global extension_mode setting for this specific extension.

notes.description: Shown in dry-run output and logs.
